# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

workflow_name: Build and Notify on PR
on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

env:
  PR_TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
  PR_BODY: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}
  PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
  PR_URL: ${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}
  PR_REF: ${{ github.ref }}
  PR_SHA: ${{ github.sha }}

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0 # Needed for some commit-related operations if you want to inspect history

      - name: Log PR Details
        run: |
          echo "PR Title: $PR_TITLE"
          echo "PR Body: $PR_BODY"
          echo "PR Number: $PR_NUMBER"
          echo "PR URL: $PR_URL"
          echo "Ref: $PR_REF"
          echo "SHA: $PR_SHA"
          # Example of using PR_TITLE for a 'commit message' or similar
          # For actual commit, you'd usually use `git commit -m "$PR_TITLE"` within a step that makes changes.
          # This just demonstrates capturing it.
          echo "Simulated commit message for release notes: $PR_TITLE (PR #$PR_NUMBER)"

      - name: Your build step goes here
        run: |
          echo "--- Starting Build Process ---"
          # Replace with your actual build commands
          echo "Building project related to PR: $PR_TITLE"
          # For example, if you have a build script:
          # ./build.sh
          # Or a npm build:
          # npm install
          # npm run build
          echo "--- Build Finished ---"

      - name: Notify on Build Status (Example with a placeholder for a notification system)
        env:
          NOTIFICATION_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.NOTIFICATION_WEBHOOK_URL }} # Store your webhook URL as a secret
        run: |
          # This is a placeholder. Replace with actual notification logic for Slack, Teams, Discord, etc.
          # Example using curl to send a simple message to a webhook:
          if [ -n "$NOTIFICATION_WEBHOOK_URL" ]; then
            echo "Sending notification about build status for PR: $PR_NUMBER - $PR_TITLE"
            # Example JSON payload for a generic webhook
            MESSAGE_PAYLOAD='{
              "text": "🚀 *New PR Build Completed!* 🚀\n\n'"
              "PR Title: *$PR_TITLE*\n"'"
              "PR Description:\n```\n$PR_BODY\n```\n"'"
              "PR Number: #$PR_NUMBER\n"'"
              "Link: <$PR_URL|View PR>\n"'"
              "Workflow: <${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}|View Workflow Run>"
            }'
            
            # Using jq to properly escape and format JSON payload
            # Replace 'webhook.site' with your actual notification system's endpoint
            # e.g., curl -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "$MESSAGE_PAYLOAD" "$NOTIFICATION_WEBHOOK_URL"
            # For demonstration, we just echo the payload
            echo "Notification Payload:"
            echo "$MESSAGE_PAYLOAD" | jq .
            echo "--- Notification sent (simulated) ---"
          else
            echo "NOTIFICATION_WEBHOOK_URL secret not set. Skipping notification."
          fi

  # You can add more jobs here, e.g., `test`, `deploy`
  # deploy:
  #   needs: build
  #   if: success() && github.event.pull_request.merged == true # Example for deploying on merge
  #   runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  #   steps:
  #     - name: Deploy Feature
  #       run: |
  #         echo "Deploying the feature related to: $PR_TITLE"
  #         # Deployment commands here
  #         echo "Deployed '$PR_TITLE' (PR #$PR_NUMBER) to staging."
  #     - name: Notify on Deployment
  #       env:
  #         NOTIFICATION_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.NOTIFICATION_WEBHOOK_URL }}
  #       run: |
  #         # ... similar notification logic for deployment ...
  #         echo "Deployment notification for $PR_TITLE sent."
